features.name: Vision to Feature Decomposition
features.description: Accept a plain-language product vision and automatically decompose it into a prioritized, dependency-ordered feature plan.
acceptance_criteria: Vision to Feature Decomposition behavior is represented in runtime artifacts and workflow execution.

features.name: Autonomous Implementation
features.description: Assign features to coding agents that write modular, tested code following project conventions — in parallel where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Autonomous Implementation behavior is represented in runtime artifacts and workflow execution.

features.name: Self-Healing Test Loop
features.description: When tests fail, a debugging agent diagnoses the root cause to propose and apply fixes, then re-validates — without human intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Self-Healing Test Loop behavior is represented in runtime artifacts and workflow execution.

features.name: Architecture Review
features.description: An architecture agent continuously evaluates repository structure, dependencies, and security posture, producing actionable reports.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture Review behavior is represented in runtime artifacts and workflow execution.

features.name: Continuous Learning
features.description: Every completed run feeds a learning agent that extracts reusable patterns, bug fixes, and architectural lessons into long-term memory.
acceptance_criteria: Continuous Learning behavior is represented in runtime artifacts and workflow execution.

features.name: Offline-First, Online-Amplified
features.description: The system works locally without internet. When connectivity is available, it unlocks remote research, dependency scanning, and cloud deployment.
acceptance_criteria: Offline-First, Online-Amplified behavior is represented in runtime artifacts and workflow execution.

features.name: Policy-Governed Safety
features.description: Every action — file writes, deployments, network access — passes through a policy engine that enforces safety rules, blocks secrets in code, and gates destructive operations. ## Non-Goals (What SkyForce Is Not) ## Success Criteria SkyForce is successful when: 1. A developer can describe a feature in plain English and receive a working, tested implementation without writing a single line of code 2. Test failures are automatically diagnosed and fixed in >70% of cases 3. No deployment can occur with failing tests or detected secrets 4. The system improves its own performance over time through learned patterns 5. Loss of internet does not stop local development work
acceptance_criteria: Not a code editor or IDE replacement
Not a project management tool (it consumes task lists, doesn't manage them)
Not a CI/CD platform (it triggers deployments, doesn't host them)
